evals.prompt: Use $uxl-onednn to plan a oneDNN matmul integration for fp32 tensors on CPU, including memory descriptors, correctness validation, and when to use benchdnn.
evals.expected_output: Selects a primitive path, names tensor shape/layout assumptions, includes reorder considerations, gives a correctness check, and avoids claiming speedup without a benchmark.

evals.prompt: Use $uxl-onednn to review a proposal to fuse convolution, bias, and ReLU before there is an unfused reference test.
evals.expected_output: Rejects fusion-first workflow, asks for or creates an unfused reference, defines tolerance, then proposes post-op or graph fusion only after parity.

evals.prompt: Use $uxl-onednn to triage a oneDNN performance regression after a layout change introduced extra reorders.
evals.expected_output: Focuses on logical versus physical layout, identifies reorder boundaries, asks for shapes/backend/build, and proposes benchdnn or project benchmark evidence.
